output.queryType: Strategic validation + go-to-market planning + build vs. buy decision

founderProfile.founderExperience: Domain expert (8 years fashion retail marketing)

extractedContext.coreProblem: Sustainable fashion brands lack a curated, trustworthy sales channel; conscious consumers struggle to find verified ethical options
extractedContext.solution: Curated marketplace with strict sustainability vetting; positioning as premium alternative to open marketplaces
statedConcerns: Competitive threat from existing marketplaces adding sustainability filters
Thin margins in sustainable fashion reducing brand willingness to pay 15% commission
High customer acquisition costs in e-commerce
openDecisions: Build custom platform vs. use Shopify marketplace tools
constraints: Founder is non-technical (though co-founder can build)

keyQuestions: What is the actual willingness-to-pay for sustainable fashion brands? Does 15% commission + $99/mo align with their unit economics, or will you face adoption resistance?
How defensible is curation as a moat? If Amazon/Etsy add sustainability filters, what prevents commoditization of your value proposition?
What is the unit economics of customer acquisition for conscious consumers? Can you achieve profitability at scale given e-commerce CAC benchmarks?
Should you launch with 10 hand-curated brands (build trust, prove model) or 100 brands (demonstrate scale, improve unit economics)? What's the minimum viable supply-side to attract demand?
Is a custom build justified at $40K budget, or does Shopify marketplace reduce time-to-market and allow capital reallocation to customer acquisition?

triage.rawText:
```json
{
  "ideaType": "B2B2C Marketplace / Curated E-commerce Platform",
  "queryType": "Strategic validation + go-to-market planning + build vs. buy decision",
  "founderProfile": {
    "teamSize": 2,
    "technicalAbility": "High (co-founder is full-stack developer)",
    "founderExperience": "Domain expert (8 years fashion retail marketing)",
    "budget": "$40K",
    "fundingStage": "Pre-seed, no external capital"
  },
  "extractedContext": {
    "coreProblem": "Sustainable fashion brands lack a curated, trustworthy sales channel; conscious consumers struggle to find verified ethical options",
    "targetMarket": "Conscious consumers + ethical fashion brands (SMEs)",
    "solution": "Curated marketplace with strict sustainability vetting; positioning as premium alternative to open marketplaces",
    "revenueModel": "15% commission + $99/month brand listing fee",
    "statedConcerns": [
      "Competitive threat from existing marketplaces adding sustainability filters",
      "Thin margins in sustainable fashion reducing brand willingness to pay 15% commission",
      "High customer acquisition costs in e-commerce"
    ],
    "openDecisions": [
      "Build custom platform vs. use Shopify marketplace tools",
      "Launch with 10 brands (quality-focused) vs. 100 brands (scale-focused)"
    ],
    "constraints": [
      "Limited budget ($40K)",
      "No external funding",
      "Founder is non-technical (though co-founder can build)"
    ]
  },
  "sectionPriorities": {
    "businessModel": "detailed",
    "marketAnalysis": "detailed",
    "competitiveStrategy": "detailed",
    "technicalFeasibility": "standard",
    "decisionAgenda": "detailed",
    "goToMarket": "detailed"
  },
  "keyQuestions": [
    "What is the actual willingness-to-pay for sustainable fashion brands? Does 15% commission + $99/mo align with their unit economics, or will you face adoption resistance?",
    "How defensible is curation as a moat? If Amazon/Etsy add sustainability filters, what prevents commoditization of your value proposition?",
    "What is the unit economics of customer acquisition for conscious consumers? Can you achieve profitability at scale given e-commerce CAC benchmarks?",
    "Should you launch with 10 hand-curated brands (build trust, prove model) or 100 brands (demonstrate scale, improve unit economics)? What's the minimum viable supply-side to attract demand?",
    "Is a custom build justified at $40K budget, or does Shopify marketplace reduce time-to-market and allow capital reallocation to customer acquisition?"
  ],
  "language": "en"
}
```

executiveSummary.summary: Your sustainable fashion marketplace faces significant structural challenges: thin margins in sustainable fashion make your 15% commission + $99/mo fee potentially prohibitive, established players like Etsy and Amazon are already adding sustainability filters, and customer acquisition costs in fashion e-commerce average $50-150. However, your fashion industry experience and the growing conscious consumer segment (25% CAGR) create a narrow window if you can solve the unit economics puzzle.

marketSize.sam: $3B US conscious consumers willing to pay premium for sustainable fashion
marketSize.som: $50M realistic addressable market for curated marketplace (assuming 3-5% market penetration)
marketSize.methodology: Based on 15% of US fashion market ($200B) being sustainability-conscious, with 10-15% willing to use specialized platforms

competitors.name: The Yes
competitors.description: Curated sustainable fashion marketplace backed by major VCs

competitors.name: Etsy
competitors.description: Handmade/vintage marketplace with growing sustainability category
competitors.strength: Massive user base, low customer acquisition cost

competitors.name: Reformation
competitors.strength: Strong brand, proven sustainable fashion business model

competitors.name: Farfetch
competitors.description: Luxury fashion marketplace adding sustainability filters
competitors.strength: Established luxury relationships, proven marketplace model

marketAnalysis.positioning: The trusted curator for mid-market sustainable fashion brands seeking conscious consumers who value authenticity over luxury
marketAnalysis.timing: Gen Z and millennial spending power is peaking, sustainability regulations are increasing brand compliance costs, making third-party verification valuable
gaps: Mid-market sustainable brands lack dedicated sales channels

valueProposition.coreProblem: Sustainable fashion brands struggle with customer acquisition while conscious consumers can't trust sustainability claims without verification
valueProposition.targetCustomer: Women 25-45, household income $75K+, shop sustainable brands 3+ times per year, frustrated by greenwashing
valueProposition.uniqueAngle: Strict sustainability verification process combined with fashion industry insider curation
valueProposition.whyNow: ESG reporting requirements make sustainability verification more valuable to brands, consumer skepticism of greenwashing is at all-time high

businessModel.revenueStrategy: Commission-based marketplace with subscription component for predictable revenue
businessModel.pricingFramework: Start with 10% commission + $49/mo to improve adoption, increase to 15% + $99/mo after proving value

unitEconomics.estimatedCAC: $75-120 for conscious consumers (fashion e-commerce average), $500-1000 for brand acquisition
unitEconomics.estimatedLTV: $180-250 customer LTV based on 3-4 purchases/year at $60 average order value
unitEconomics.paybackPeriod: 12-18 months for consumers, 6-12 months for brands

businessModel.scalabilityAssessment: At 10x scale, curation becomes bottleneck unless systematized. Network effects weak until 50+ brands and 10K+ active customers.

technicalFeasibility.recommendedStack: Shopify Plus marketplace solution for MVP, migrate to custom Rails/React if proven

buildVsBuy.rationale: Shopify Plus costs $2K/mo but saves 4-6 months development time, critical given budget constraints
Core differentiator that requires custom logic for sustainability criteria
Stripe Connect handles marketplace payments, regulatory compliance included

technicalRisks: Shopify marketplace limitations as you scale
technicalFeasibility.estimatedMvpTimeline: 3-4 months with Shopify Plus, 8-12 months custom build

risks.description: Established marketplaces like Etsy and Amazon adding sustainability filters eliminates your differentiation
risks.mitigation: Build deep brand relationships and exclusive partnerships that can't be replicated by algorithms

risks.description: 15% commission + $99/mo may be too expensive for sustainable fashion brands with 40-50% gross margins
risks.mitigation: Survey 20 target brands about pricing sensitivity before launch, consider tiered pricing

risks.description: Customer acquisition costs in fashion e-commerce ($75-150) may exceed customer lifetime value
risks.mitigation: Focus on organic growth through brand partnerships and influencer marketing rather than paid acquisition

risks.description: Venture-backed competitors like The Yes have deeper pockets for brand acquisition and marketing
risks.mitigation: Focus on underserved mid-market segment where you can compete on personal relationships

week1to4: Survey 20 sustainable fashion brands about commission sensitivity and marketplace needs
Interview 50 conscious consumers about shopping behavior and platform preferences
Set up basic Shopify Plus marketplace instance
week5to8: Recruit first 5 brands with personal relationships from fashion industry
Build brand application and verification workflow
Create content marketing strategy targeting conscious consumers
week9to12: Implement basic analytics tracking for conversion rates
Test customer acquisition channels: Instagram, Pinterest, sustainable fashion blogs

decisionAgenda.question: What commission rate and monthly fee can sustainable fashion brands actually afford?

options.tradeoff: Higher revenue per brand vs. potential adoption resistance
Better adoption vs. lower unit economics

options.option: Tiered pricing based on brand size
options.tradeoff: Complexity vs. broader market appeal

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with 10% + $49/mo to prove value, then increase pricing for new brands after demonstrating ROI

decisionAgenda.question: Should you build custom platform or use Shopify Plus marketplace?

options.tradeoff: Full control and differentiation vs. 8-12 month timeline and higher costs
Faster launch and lower costs vs. platform limitations and ongoing fees

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Use Shopify Plus for MVP to preserve capital for customer acquisition, migrate later if proven

decisionAgenda.question: Launch with 10 brands (quality) or 100 brands (scale)?

options.tradeoff: Higher quality and trust vs. limited selection and inventory risk
Better selection and inventory vs. curation quality concerns

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Start with 10 exceptional brands to establish trust and prove curation value, expand to 50 within 6 months

decisionAgenda.question: What customer acquisition strategy can work within your budget constraints?

options.tradeoff: Scalable reach vs. high CAC ($75-150)
Lower costs and higher trust vs. slower scaling
Leveraged reach vs. dependency on brand relationships

decisionAgenda.recommendation: Focus on influencer partnerships and brand co-marketing to minimize cash burn while building authentic audience
